review_text: Sub/titles are so distracting. Trying to speed read causes one to miss a lot of the emotion. Pass.

You can like this movie and not be a snob. Likewise, you can hate this movie and not be media illiterate. Amélie is a young, introverted lady. She communicates through elaborate prank systems. Positive pranks for people she likes and negative pranks for people she thinks need to learn a lesson. The movie can feel a bit directionless at times. The pranks are not all that endearing. The humor is a mixed bag, some of them absolutely nail it, others not so much. The romance isn't as much a focus. It is more the idea of giving romance a chance, putting yourself out there and seeing what makes you happy. It is a movie with a lot of good moments, but one that doesn't necessarily tie them all together. Still, it did make me smile.
Yeah, I've seen this. Pretty iconic in the world of film. I would watch it again.
A cult classic , a must see for lovers of cinematic art
One of my favourites. Just watched it and still holds up wonderfully.

I saw the film at the cinema in 2001, and watched it again in 2025, during a cinema re-release. I no longer remember what I thought of it back in 2001. This time, I found it interesting — the love story between them is well constructed.
I'm only here because not only was this a great movie, Amelie looks just like the main character girl in Alien Earth haha

I get why critics and audiences highly regard Amelie; however, it simply fell short of meeting my expectations. The film's whimsical charm is offset by the mediocre acting. The film's beautiful score is drowned out in the orange filter shrouding every scene. And the film's emotional message is lost amidst the quirky humor. I guess this one just wasn't for me.
Fantastic movie. Made me reconnect with my inner self.
My favourite film ever.
As years go by, I always come back to a line from this movie: “To a kid, time always drags. Suddenly you’re fifty. All that’s left of your childhood… fits in a rusty little box.”
Still one of my favourite movies.

Quite honestly, the best film ever. Number one in my All-Time-Top-10-Films. It's perfect! I guess it's a wacky Romance. As a non French speaker It's not easy reading the subtitles as they come thick and fast. But it's totally worth the effort. The French sounds exquisite and in my head I always forget that it's a subtitled film. To describe the cinematography, acting, story, direction, art direction, camerawork and casting I would only use one word. Nothing describes them better than - 'Perfect'. I've seen it so many times and look forward to the many times more I will watch it. The only thing I haven't mentioned are the FX. Unsurprisingly - I couldn't love them more!

Amélie is not merely a film—it is an enchanted diorama of the soul, constructed with lace-gloved hands for the secretly radiant, the romantically wounded, and the quietly mad. Directed with the precision of a jeweler crafting dreams, it’s a cinematic confection wrapped in Parisian nostalgia, dipped in whimsy, and dusted with a melancholy only the beautiful can taste. Its colours don’t just pop—they breathe, sigh, and wink at those still capable of tenderness in a brutal world. This world doesn’t open to the jaded or the joyless; if you feel locked out, mon cher, it is your limitations rattling the gates, not the film's. Amélie is a sacred invitation: either you see the magic, or you never deserved to.

This is one of my favourite films. I can understand why people don't like it. But I like the exaggerated romatincisation of Paris, the quirky humour, and of course the Yann Tiersen soundtrack. Amélie is a charming exploration of loneliness, kindness, and the quiet magic found in everyday life, following a young woman who brightens the world of those around her while grappling with her own longing for love and connection. Through its playful visual style and whimsical narrative, the film celebrates imagination, small acts of goodness, and the courage it takes to break free from isolation and embrace vulnerability. Ultimately, it reminds us that happiness often comes when we dare to step beyond our comfort zones and open ourselves to others.
Since it came out my go to movie whenever i feel down. A modern classic with nostalgic, dreamy atmosphere with one of the best movie soundtracks.

I will say right away that this film has more artistic value than entertainment value. For a bit it felt like the film dragged on too long and it made me a little uninterested, however I understood some of its significance. The actress who played Amelie did a very good job and there were quite a few comedic moments in the movie as well. It was also good to see more vulnerable and deeper character moments, plus certain reveals that were interesting. It’s hard to believe that this director made one of the worst movies in the Alien franchise before he went on to make this movie (which used pretty captivating directing techniques)! Overall, not my typical kind of movie but it for sure wasn’t bad!
I watched this when it came out in 2001 and again in 2025. It’s still just as charming. What a great movie.
Wonderful directing, proto-Manic Pixie Dream Girl, problematic romance IMO
Unforgettable, magical, all of it...you don t even need to read the subtitles to feel what it gives you in abundantly.
Bouleversant, transportant de poésie et de tendresse. Passionnément humain et profond. Merveilleux film français. À voir.

Amélie consegue ser cativante e delicada com uma essência sonhadora e altruísta, seus atos de bondade são um momeno de pura satisfação. Assistir um filme cheio de pequenos detalhes, toques de humor e conseguir criar uma conexão emocional é realmente revigorante, celebrar as pequenas alegrias da vida se mostra a mehor forma de ver o mundo.
Perfect feel good film. Seen it so many times and it's always enchanting. Amazing direction and feels like a book come to life.

It's been more than 20 years since the premiere.... I watched in the cinema and was captivated. Wonderfully realized, perfectly played characters, beautiful, a little unreal shots - everything that is missing in today's films... Great ideas, unusual shots and such a serene atmosphere. This is still an excellent film: warm, optimistic, a fairy tale from a Paris that no longer exists... Worth of rewatching!

Music carries the whimsical nature of Amelies dream life. We watch her strategize ways to better the lives of unhappy people never realizing she also needs an intervention to bring fulfillment to herself. A French view of the world that cannot help but bring humor and irony to a delightful story of making things happen.
